titleEn: Joaquín García Carrasco, 1st José Manuel Esteve Prize
excerptEn: On 31 October 2011, the 1st JOSÉ MANUEL ESTEVE PRIZE was awarded to Mr Joaquín García Carrasco, Professor of Theory and History of Education and Director of the Research Unit of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca until his retirement. After the…
contentEn:
On 31 October 2011, the 1st JOSÉ MANUEL ESTEVE PRIZE was awarded to Mr Joaquín García Carrasco, Professor of Theory and History of Education and Director of the Research Unit of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca until his retirement.

After assessing the 14 articles submitted, the jury unanimously awarded the 1st JOSÉ MANUEL ESTEVE PRIZE to Mr Joaquín García Carrasco for his article entitled “Cultural maturity and lifelong education in the information society”, on account of the depth of its analysis, the topicality of its subject matter, the relevance of its sources and the expository quality of the text — to which, moreover, its pedagogical depth must be added. The article was published in the journal Bordón, 2010, 63(3).

Below you can watch the last interview given by Dr García Carrasco, on the occasion of the Third International Meeting on ICT and Education held in Uruguay in August 2011, in which he presents the results of his latest research.

excerptEn: In contexts of economic crisis such as the current one, the equitable distribution, efficient management and responsible use of the available resources take on greater relevance. It is therefore essential to seek sources of funding external to the universities, as well as efficient management of resources by higher education…
contentEn:
In contexts of economic crisis such as the current one, the equitable distribution, efficient management and responsible use of the available resources take on greater relevance. It is therefore essential to seek sources of funding external to the universities, as well as efficient management of resources by higher education institutions, within the framework of accountability proper to the Public Administrations of democratic States.

And it is in this field that the UNESCO Chair in University Management and Policy presents, on 30 November 2011 in Madrid, the second day of the Biennial Seminar “In Pursuit of Active Education”, which addresses the inefficiencies, both economic and educational, of the Spanish university system and of Spanish society. During the session, entitled “The current inefficiencies of higher education”, different stakeholders and experts will debate these issues and offer some ideas on the priorities to be addressed in the present times.

titleEn: Start of the PDI Training Programme
excerptEn: On 28 November, in the Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Translation and Documentation, the University Teaching Staff Training Plan for the current 2011/2012 academic year will get under way. The Reading Universities Network and the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching of the University of Salamanca are organising the activity “Copyright at the…
contentEn:
On 28 November, in the Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Translation and Documentation, the University Teaching Staff Training Plan for the current 2011/2012 academic year will get under way.

The Reading Universities Network and the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching of the University of Salamanca are organising the activity “Copyright at the University”, which will present the cases in which copyright and Intellectual Property legislation must be taken into account in teaching and research practice.

excerptEn: The Department of Development and the Environment of the Regional Government of Castilla y León and the CEDETEL Foundation are organising the Technology Outreach Meeting, which will take place in Salamanca on 17 November 2011 in Room 16 of the IUCE. This meeting is part of a series of 9 Provincial Technology Outreach Meetings,…
contentEn:
The Department of Development and the Environment of the Regional Government of Castilla y León and the CEDETEL Foundation are organising the Technology Outreach Meeting, which will take place in Salamanca on 17 November 2011 in Room 16 of the IUCE.

This meeting is part of a series of 9 Provincial Technology Outreach Meetings, which are completely free of charge and are aimed at the stakeholders involved in the Technology Outreach process, as well as at anyone wishing to find out about the opportunities offered by ICT: greater professional productivity, new leisure options, time savings,…

For further information, see the programme.

titleEn: Conclusion of the 2011 PDI Training Plan
excerptEn: On Wednesday 7 September, this year's edition of the 2011 University Teaching Staff Training Plan came to an end. Since March, the IUCE has run a total of 92 activities delivering more than 700 hours of training. In this way, a total of…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 7 September, this year's edition of the 2011 University Teaching Staff Training Plan came to an end.

Since March, the IUCE has run a total of 92 activities delivering more than 700 hours of training. In this way, a total of 1,818 places were allocated, covering more than 60% of the demand received.

Worth highlighting is the aim of familiarising teaching staff with the teaching methodologies and the student assessment process involved in the European Higher Education Area, with particular emphasis on the use of the Studium virtual platform and on the use of IT tools and digital audiovisual technology in teaching practice.

As an important part of this programme, we highlight the 2011 Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff, which aims to equip new lecturers with the competences they need to begin their careers as university teaching professionals.

Pictured are some of the students who successfully completed the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) in this edition, together with Mª José Rodríguez Conde, director of the Institute.

excerptEn: From next Wednesday the 14th until Friday the 17th of this month, the International Conference on Methods and Technologies for Learning in Contexts will take place in Salamanca. This activity, organised by the GRIAL Research Group, is part of the European project MIH – Multicultural Interdisciplinary Handbook: tools for Learning History and…
contentEn:
From next Wednesday the 14th until Friday the 17th of this month, the International Conference “Methods and technologies for learning in contexts” will take place in Salamanca. This activity, organised by the GRIAL Research Group, is part of the European project MIH – Multicultural Interdisciplinary Handbook: tools for Learning History and Geography in a Multicultural Perspective (ref. 502461-LLP-1-2009-1-ES-COMENIUSCMP), and also has the support of various European partner universities from Austria, France, Germany, Italy and Poland.

The activity will take place at the Hospedería of the Colegio Fonseca. All the information is available on its website.

excerptEn: As part of the USAL Summer Courses catalogue, the course “Learning with technology. The SMART Board interactive whiteboard” was held on 30 June and 1 July 2011. The course, directed by María José Rodríguez Conde and coordinated by Fernando Martínez Abad and Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos, was delivered…
contentEn:
As part of the USAL Summer Courses catalogue, the course “Learning with technology. The SMART Board interactive whiteboard” was held on 30 June and 1 July 2011. The course, directed by María José Rodríguez Conde and coordinated by Fernando Martínez Abad and Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos, was delivered at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and was sponsored by SMART Board.

In addition to the contribution of the organisers, the course enjoyed the internal collaboration of Francisco J. García Peñalvo (director of the GRIAL research group) and Susana Olmos (a national point of reference when it comes to assessment); and, externally, of two schoolteachers: Mónica Gutierrez (C.E.I.P. San Sebastián, El Boalo, Madrid) and Mar Ayuela (C.P.I.P Marqués de Santillana, Palencia).

titleEn: New Anatomy lessons, in Tribuna de la Ciencia
excerptEn: The work of the team led by Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, a scientist at the Department of Human Anatomy and Histology and Deputy Director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), carried out in collaboration with various specialists from all over Spain, features on the front cover of issue 54, June 2011, of the journal Tribuna de la…
contentEn: The work of the team led by Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, a scientist at the Department of Human Anatomy and Histology and Deputy Director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), carried out in collaboration with various specialists from all over Spain, features on the front cover of issue 54, June 2011, of the journal Tribuna de la Ciencia, a monthly publication for technology researchers. The journal devotes pages 2 and 3 to these 3D anatomical viewers, which have been created both for medical training and for surgical procedures.

excerptEn: The Deputy Director of the IUCE, Prof. Dr Juan A. Juanes Méndez, took part on 13 May, as an invited speaker, in the Teaching Technology Conference organised by the University of Barcelona, with a lecture entitled “Technological tools for teaching innovation to optimise and improve learning”. In his lecture, Professor Juanes described…
contentEn:
The Deputy Director of the IUCE, Prof. Dr Juan A. Juanes Méndez, took part on 13 May, as an invited speaker, in the Teaching Technology Conference organised by the University of Barcelona, with a lecture entitled “Technological tools for teaching innovation to optimise and improve learning”.

In his lecture, Professor Juanes described new training resources and environments and innovative teaching methodologies that facilitate and optimise student learning.

He presented practical examples of three fundamental aspects:

1. Tools for generating animated 3D images for teaching purposes (Poser, Maya, 3D Studio, ZBrush, Blender, Second Life…).

2. Technological procedures for the audiovisual transmission of teaching content (virtual campuses, video streaming, Livestream, Producer, tele-immersion, chroma key technology).

3. Virtual teaching-learning environments. Medical simulation systems using computer-based procedures (virtual classrooms, virtual consultations and virtual patients, clinical trainers: auscultation, ultrasound examination, surgical approaches with augmented reality…).

He concluded the lecture by pointing out that the use of technological methods for training and for the visualisation of knowledge, and the exploration of their teaching possibilities, is a field “within our responsibility as teachers, and a constant challenge that we must face, both those of us working in education and those responsible for creating and developing technological applications to optimise learning”.

excerptEn: Last week, on 16, 17 and 18 May, the project “ISABEL. Interactive social media for integration, skills exchange, training and informal learning” was presented at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE). A project funded by the European Commission and EACEA (the Education, Audiovisual and…
contentEn:
Last week, on 16, 17 and 18 May, the project “ISABEL. Interactive social media for integration, skills exchange, training and informal learning” was presented at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE). A project funded by the European Commission and EACEA (the Education, Audiovisual and Culture Executive Agency), with an estimated duration of two years.

This project aims to develop an innovative pedagogical practice, based on social networks and on so-called citizen journalism, that serves as a tool for learning and empowerment, both for groups of individuals and for communities. To this end, the intention is to create a virtual and physical environment where all kinds of people can find a way to learn reciprocally from one another, thereby reducing their vulnerabilities. The aim is thus to respond to the need to foster self-learning and informal peer-to-peer education processes.

titleEn: Visit by the Director of Communication of the Regional Government to the IUCE
excerptEn: The Director of Communication of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, Dr Ángel Losada Vázquez, visited the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) for the preparatory working meeting of the Campus de Cossio, organised by the Department of the Presidency of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, in…
contentEn: The Director of Communication of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, Dr Ángel Losada Vázquez, visited the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) for the preparatory working meeting of the Campus de Cossio, organised by the Department of the Presidency of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, in which several universities of the Autonomous Community of Castilla y León take part. During the visit, possible avenues of collaboration between the IUCE and the Directorate of Communication of the Regional Government were also explored.

titleEn: Ultrasound anatomical viewer for regional anaesthesia
excerptEn: On 5 May 2011, the software tool Ultrasound anatomical viewer for regional anaesthesia was presented at the Palacio de Congresos of the Community of Madrid, at the 30th National and 14th Spanish-Portuguese Congress of the Spanish Society of Anaesthesiology, Resuscitation and Pain Therapy, within the Symposium organised by Abbott Laboratories. The…
contentEn:
On 5 May 2011, the software tool Ultrasound anatomical viewer for regional anaesthesia was presented at the Palacio de Congresos of the Community of Madrid, at the 30th National and 14th Spanish-Portuguese Congress of the Spanish Society of Anaesthesiology, Resuscitation and Pain Therapy, within the Symposium organised by Abbott Laboratories.

The Deputy Director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, lecturer in the Human Anatomy Area of the Faculty of Medicine of the University of Salamanca and principal investigator of the Research Group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (“VisualMed System”), took part, together with other renowned professionals, in the software development of the tool, whose computing work was carried out with the collaboration of “Desarrollos informáticos Abadía”, of Madrid.

Related news:

    University of Salamanca lecturers create a new training tool in the field of anaesthesia. USAL Communication Unit – 16/05/2011

    University scientists design an anatomical viewer unique in the world. La Gaceta Regional de Salamanca -17/05/2011

    The University and the Hospital create an unprecedented training tool for anaesthesia. La Gaceta Regional de Salamanca -17/05/2011

excerptEn: The 1st International Conference on Collaborative Learning Methodologies through ICT will take place on 20, 21 and 22 June 2011 at the Faculty of Education of the University of Salamanca. It is a meeting open to all teachers at every level of education, in which…
contentEn:
The 1st International Conference on Collaborative Learning Methodologies through ICT will take place on 20, 21 and 22 June 2011 at the Faculty of Education of the University of Salamanca. It is a meeting open to all teachers at every level of education, featuring debates among specialists who carry out research or develop practical experiences in implementing collaborative learning methodologies through the use of ICT.

The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) is one of the organisations collaborating in this conference, which is also recognised with 2 free-elective credits.

All the information on the programme, registration and the submission of papers is available at the following links: http://www.cimac2011.blogspot.com/; the Facebook page Congreso Internacional CIMAC, and Twitter @cimac2011.

excerptEn: Over the previous months, the fourth-year students of Audiovisual Communication produced promotional adverts devoted to different faculties and degrees. “All the fourth-year students have taken part in the activity, working in groups of three to five people,” explains Félix Ortega, lecturer in Audiovisual Communication and Academic Secretary…
contentEn:
Over the previous months, the fourth-year students of Audiovisual Communication produced promotional adverts devoted to different faculties and degrees. “All the fourth-year students have taken part in the activity, working in groups of three to five people,” explains Félix Ortega, lecturer in Audiovisual Communication and Academic Secretary of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE).

On Thursday 17 March, the Auditorium of the FES Building (on the Unamuno Campus) hosted the presentation gala for these short films at the “2nd USAL Adverts Competition”, at which the students themselves, by ballot, voted for and chose the best videos — videos intended to capture the prevailing spirit of the different faculties and centres they could choose. “They were given the chance to choose for themselves which degrees and which faculties they were going to cover,” adds Professor Ortega.

Among the degrees and faculties chosen were: Geology, Sociology, Medicine, Criminology, Philology, English Philology, Chemistry, Biology, Romance Language, Literature and Cultures, Fine Arts, Philosophy, the University of Experience and Audiovisual Communication. Those attending the event had the chance to vote for the three adverts they liked best. From this anonymous ballot the winners emerged: in third place, Fine Arts; in second place, Criminology; and crowning the podium, in first position, the advert for Audiovisual Communication.

The report produced by UsalTV and all the adverts can be enjoyed here.

excerptEn: The 2011 Teaching Training Plan for the teaching staff of the University of Salamanca is an initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching, developed by the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), setting out an institutional proposal aimed at all teaching staff with the objective of making it easier for lecturers to…
contentEn:
The 2011 Teaching Training Plan for the teaching staff of the University of Salamanca is an initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching, developed by the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), setting out an institutional proposal aimed at all teaching staff with the objective of making it easier for lecturers to adopt the teaching methodologies required by the current official degree programmes.

The 2010-11 academic year began with the complete adaptation of all degree programmes to the new university context of Bachelor's, Master's and Doctoral studies, with each centre having its own distinct characteristics. From this starting point, this Plan seeks to respond to this diversity by proposing a threefold training strategy:

  • General Training Programme: a general offer proposed by the IUCE, with the collaboration of the centres.
  • In-Centre Training Programme: specific training linked to particular needs identified in each centre.
  • Open Training Programme: a dynamic offer to which institutional activities linked to the updating of teaching practice may be added.

The warm reception given to the activities organised in previous editions, the positive evaluations and the recognition of the participants are an encouragement to continue this work. This programme, which has been consolidating over recent years, remains open to any suggestions put forward by teaching staff, Departments and Centres, whom we invite to enrich this call and to help extend it in future editions.

In the hope that the proposals contained in this call will meet the training needs of our university community, we once again encourage everyone to take part in the programme presented here. Pre-registration for all the activities will be possible from 14 March at 10 a.m. until Friday the 18th at 2 p.m. through the platform that will be made available on this website, with the exception of the course “Initial Training for University Teaching Staff (FIPU)”, for which registration can be completed on Thursday 10 March by emailing iuce.formacion@usal.es

excerptEn: The EVALtrends 2011 International Conference, held in Cádiz from 9 to 11 March 2011, focused on the topic “Assessment for learning at university: innovative experiences”. It took shape as a forum for debate offering the opportunity to contribute, share and disseminate innovative experiences and…
contentEn: The International Conference EVALtrends 2011, held in Cádiz from 9 to 11 March 2011, focused on the topic “Assessment for learning at university: innovative experiences”. It took shape as a forum for debate offering the opportunity to contribute, share and disseminate innovative experiences and practices that put forward useful and viable alternatives for the assessment of competences and their learning outcomes at university. The publication of the contributions (still awaiting the conclusions) is available as an open-access PDF on the conference home page.

excerptEn: The publisher from Peñaranda Germán Sánchez Ruipérez received on 10 February 2011 the highest distinction awarded by the University of Salamanca, the degree of Doctor Honoris Causa, in a ceremony presided over by the Rector of the academic institution, Daniel Hernández Ruipérez, and whose conferral was approved on 28 December by the Assembly of…
contentEn:
The publisher from Peñaranda Germán Sánchez Ruipérez received on 10 February 2011 the highest distinction awarded by the University of Salamanca, the degree of Doctor Honoris Causa, in a ceremony presided over by the Rector of the academic institution, Daniel Hernández Ruipérez, and whose conferral was approved on 28 December by the Assembly of Doctors.

At the ceremony, held in the Paraninfo of the Escuelas Mayores of the University, the new doctor was accompanied by his sponsor, María José Rodríguez Conde, director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), from which his candidacy was put forward. Numerous authorities, family members and friends were also present, wishing to share such a special moment with him.

During his speech, in which he was visibly moved, Germán Sánchez Ruipérez described himself as a “man of publishing and of books”. This was something he had in common with Stephen Whitaker, scientist and professor at the University of California, who also received the distinction of doctor honoris causa, becoming the first chemical engineer to obtain it from the University of Salamanca.

titleEn: Honorary Doctorate for Germán Sánchez Ruipérez
excerptEn: The publisher from Peñaranda Germán Sánchez Ruipérez received on 10 February 2011 the highest distinction awarded by the University of Salamanca, the degree of Doctor Honoris Causa, in a ceremony presided over by the Rector of the academic institution, Daniel Hernández Ruipérez, and whose conferral was approved on 28 December by the Assembly of…
contentEn:
The publisher from Peñaranda Germán Sánchez Ruipérez received on 10 February 2011 the highest distinction awarded by the University of Salamanca, the Doctor Honoris Causa, in a ceremony presided over by the Rector of the academic institution, Daniel Hernández Ruipérez, and whose conferral was approved on 28 December by the Assembly of Doctors. His sponsor was Dr María José Rodríguez Conde, director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), from which his candidacy was put forward. Chronicle of the investiture ceremony.

 

Presentation of the book “Delinquency, Reformatory and Liberating Education”.

The event featured Dr José Ortega Esteban, Professor of Social Pedagogy at the University of Salamanca and author of the book, Dr Jaume Trilla Bernet, Professor of Theory and History of Education at the Autonomous University of Barcelona and author of the book's prologue, and Dr María José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the IUCE, University of Salamanca.

At the end of the event, José Ortega Esteban was presented with a plaque as a memento and in gratitude for all the moments shared at the IUCE.

Commemorative concert for the 25th Anniversary of the Chamber Choir of the University of Salamanca, held on 23 April 2010 in the Auditorium of the Hospedería Fonseca.

excerptEn: On 1 February 2011, the final interview was held with the first-year students of the Degree in Computer Engineering in Information Systems in the Computer Systems module at the Higher Polytechnic School of Zamora, as part of the Innovation Project “Re-engineering of e-Assessment, technologies and competence development in…
contentEn:
On 1 February 2011, the final interview was held with the first-year students of the Degree in Computer Engineering in Information Systems in the Computer Systems module at the Higher Polytechnic School of Zamora, as part of the Innovation Project “Re-engineering of e-Assessment, technologies and competence development in professionals and university students” in coordination with several Spanish universities.

The purpose of this interview is to obtain qualitative information from the students regarding their acquisition of general competences through the Re-Evalúa project. It is an interview that complements the information gathered through a more quantitative self-report.

titleEn: Visit by CEDETEL to the IUCE
excerptEn: On 31 January 2011, a working meeting was held between the management team of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and members of the management of the Telecommunications Development Centre (CEDETEL) with the aim of strengthening the institutional relationship and launching new joint research projects.…
contentEn:
On 31 January 2011, a working meeting was held between the management team of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and members of the management of the Telecommunications Development Centre (CEDETEL) with the aim of strengthening the institutional relationship and launching new joint research projects.

The meeting was attended by:

  • María José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the IUCE.
  • Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, Deputy Director of the IUCE.
  • Félix Ortega Mohedano, Academic Secretary of the IUCE.
  • Raúl Sanz Guadarrama, Director of Technology Transfer, CEDETEL.
  • Álvar Aragón Berengena, Head of the OTRI, CEDETEL.
  • Paloma Rojo Aparicio, Head of the Public Administrations Line, CEDETEL.

titleEn: Presentation of the Biciaventura Solidaria Documentary
excerptEn: Ms Noemí Domínguez García, Vice-Rector for International and Institutional Relations, Ms Ana Cuevas Badallo, Secretary General, and Ms Emma Camarero Calandria, Director of the Documentary and Lecturer in Audiovisual Communication at the University of Salamanca, presented the documentary “Ethiopia, Forgotten Hopes”. The audiovisual document presented shows the 7th stage of the round-the…
contentEn:
Ms Noemí Domínguez García, Vice-Rector for International and Institutional Relations, Ms Ana Cuevas Badallo, Secretary General, and Ms Emma Camarero Calandria, Director of the Documentary and Lecturer in Audiovisual Communication at the University of Salamanca, presented the documentary “Ethiopia, Forgotten Hopes”.

The audiovisual document presented shows the 7th stage of the round-the-world cycling journey undertaken by the members of Biciaventura Solidaria (website). Having covered such disparate places as Istanbul, Jerusalem, India and Nepal in previous editions, on this occasion the 12 members of the team cycled almost 1,500 km across Ethiopia, with the solidarity-driven aim of improving the living conditions of children at risk of exclusion in one of the country's poorest regions, scarred by famine and the recent armed conflicts.

Thanks to the donations of the sponsors collaborating with this project, “Biciaventura Solidaria” will fund the upkeep of 150 children at the Saint Mary orphanage, run by the Spanish missionary Angel Olarán, and will provide microloans to families to develop beekeeping and livestock farming in the northern town of Wukro.

The presentation took place on the evening of 15 December 2010 in the Auditorium of the Hospedería Fonseca before an audience of approximately 100 people.

excerptEn: The IUCE, as a collaborating member, is pleased to invite all its members to the premiere of the documentary “Ethiopia, Forgotten Hopes”, to be held in the Auditorium of the Hospedería Fonseca on Wednesday 15 December 2010 at 7 p.m.
contentEn: The IUCE, as a collaborating member, is pleased to invite all its members to the premiere of the documentary “Ethiopia, Forgotten Hopes”, to be held in the Auditorium of the Hospedería Fonseca on Wednesday 15 December 2010 at 7 p.m.

excerptEn: Four books coordinated by IUCE researchers within the AECID cooperation projects with the University of Costa Rica and the UNA.
contentEn:
Between 2009 and 2010, within the framework of the cooperation projects funded by the AECID, the IUCE published the following books:

2010

Rodríguez Conde, M.ª J. y Nieto Martín, S. (coords.) (2010). Investigación y evaluación educativa en la Sociedad del Conocimiento. Salamanca: Ediciones Universidad de Salamanca.
Funded by the AECID, project B/0119587/08 “FEMINE Project. Training of Educators in Research and Evaluation Methodology. CIDE (UNA) – IUCE (USAL)”.
Download the table of contents (PDF)

2009

Salazar Salazar, S. F. y Sánchez Gómez, M.ª C. (coords.) (2009). Investigación y Formación. Teoría y práctica de la investigación educativa en la formación de educadores. Vol. 1. Manual de grado. San José, Costa Rica: Universidad de Costa Rica.
Funded by the AECID, project A/522/06 “Training of Researchers and Technicians in Content Construction for the Education System”.
Download the table of contents (PDF)

Martín García, A. V. y Venegas Renauld, M.ª E. (2009). Investigación y Formación. Teoría y práctica de la investigación educativa en la formación de educadores. Vol. 2. Manual de posgrado. San José, Costa Rica: Universidad de Costa Rica.
Funded by the AECID, project A/522/06 “Training of Researchers and Technicians in Content Construction for the Education System”.
Download the table of contents (PDF)

Salazar Salazar, S. F. y Revuelta Domínguez, F. (coords.) (2009). La Docencia Universitaria en los Espacios Virtuales. San José, Costa Rica: Universidad de Costa Rica.
Funded by the AECID, project D/6087/06 “Design of Online Courses and Professional Teaching Development”.

excerptEn: On Wednesday 17 November 2010, the ordinary session of the IUCE Council was held with the following items on the agenda: Approval, where applicable, of the minutes of the previous session. Report by the Director. Report on the agreements of the Standing Committee. Report on the 2009/10 PDI Training Programme…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 17 November 2010, the ordinary session of the IUCE Council was held with the following items on the agenda:

  • Approval, where applicable, of the minutes of the previous session.
  • Report by the Director.
  • Report on the agreements of the Standing Committee.
  • Report on the 2009/10 PDI Training Programme and the 2010/11 PDI Programme.
  • Approval, where applicable, of the 2010 financial report.
  • Report on additions to and departures from the IUCE membership.
  • Approval, where applicable, of the draft reform of the IUCE Regulations (Art 21. IUCE Regulations, 2005)
  • Any other business.

The session took place in room 16 of the IUCE, at 6:00 p.m. on first call and at 6:30 p.m. on second call.
